title: Dog Umbilical Hernia: Guide to Lumps on Your Dog's Belly
meta_desc: Notice a soft lump on your dog's belly button? Learn about umbilical hernias, when they are emergencies, and how to help your pet today.
content_html:
What is an Umbilical Hernia?

An umbilical hernia occurs when there is a small opening in the abdominal muscle wall at the site of the belly button (umbilicus). This allows abdominal fat or, in more rare cases, internal organs to protrude, creating a soft, squishy lump on your dog's belly. These are often congenital, meaning they are present from birth, and are quite common in puppies.

Is it an Emergency?

The urgency level for a standard umbilical hernia is Low. In most cases, these lumps are painless and can be reduced (pushed back in gently). Most veterinarians recommend monitoring the site and opting for a surgical repair during a routine procedure like a spay or neuter. However, if the lump suddenly becomes hard, warm, painful, or changes color, or if your dog is vomiting or lethargic, seek veterinary care immediately, as this could indicate a strangulated hernia.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a clear photo of the lump to a veterinary professional is an excellent first step in triage. A visual allows a professional to assess the size, location, and skin condition surrounding the hernia. This helps determine if the lump is a simple hernia that can wait for a scheduled check-up or if it is something more concerning, like an abscess or a tumor, that requires faster intervention.

faqs.question: Will my puppy's umbilical hernia close on its own?
faqs.answer: Small hernias in puppies under six months of age may close spontaneously as the abdominal muscles strengthen. If it hasn't closed by the time they are ready for spaying or neutering, surgical repair is usually recommended.

faqs.question: Can I push the lump back in myself?
faqs.answer: If the hernia is 'reducible,' you may be able to gently push the contents back into the abdomen. You should never force it. If the lump feels firm or stuck, do not attempt to push it in and contact your vet.

faqs.question: Is umbilical hernia surgery expensive?
faqs.answer: When performed at the same time as a spay or neuter, the cost is typically much lower than a standalone surgery because the pet is already under anesthesia.
